# ai:restore Chroma preserve-live parity for #importMemories (follow-up to #11141)

# ai:restore Chroma preserve-live parity for #importMemories (follow-up to #11141)

## Premise

#11141 corrects `Memory_DatabaseService.#importGraph` merge-mode semantics from `INSERT OR REPLACE` â†’ `INSERT OR IGNORE` (preserve-live for graph SQLite). The Chroma side (`#importMemories` for both `mc/memory-backup-*.jsonl` and `mc/summaries-backup-*.jsonl`) currently uses `collection.upsert(...)` â€” Chroma's equivalent of replace-like behavior for existing IDs.

Per @neo-gpt's #11141 peer-review (commentId 4416007918): *"`collection.upsert(...)` is the Chroma equivalent of replace-like behavior for existing ids. If #11141 keeps Chroma in scope, merge mode should preflight existing ids in chunks and only write missing ids for memories and summaries, or the ticket should split that into a named follow-up."*

#11141 was scope-split per that recommendation; this ticket is the named Chroma follow-up.

## Prescription

In `Memory_DatabaseService.#importMemories` (and `#importSummaries` if separate):

1. Add `mode` parameter handling. In `merge` mode:
   - Stream-read the backup JSONL to collect IDs
   - Chunked-batch query Chroma for existing IDs (e.g., 1000-at-a-time `collection.get({ids: [...]})`)
   - Filter to backup-only IDs (not in live)
   - Call `collection.add(...)` (insert-only) instead of `upsert(...)` for the missing-ID subset
2. In `replace` mode: keep current `upsert(...)` (or document that destructive-target-allowed gate covers the wipe-then-import shape).
3. Verify summaries follow same pattern.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `#importMemories` merge mode preflights live IDs + writes missing only.
- [ ] Same pattern for summaries.
- [ ] Unit test: ID collision in merge mode preserves live record (does not overwrite).
- [ ] Performance: chunked batching keeps per-restore time under 2x current upsert path. **[L4-deferred â€” operator handoff needed]** â€” real-Chroma perf observation requires a representative production backup + live cluster. Annotated per @neo-gpt's #11921 review (2026-05-24): "annotate #11144 with explicit `[L4-deferred â€” operator handoff needed]` residual." Close-with-residual is the substrate-correct shape; the chunked code path lands behind this AC, and the perf observation is captured post-merge against a real bundle.
- [ ] PR body cross-links #11141 (parent) + commentId 4416007918 (peer-review anchor).

## Contract Ledger

*(Added 2026-05-24 per @neo-gpt's #11921 review; documents the consumed-service return shape extension that #11921 ships.)*

| Surface | Source of Authority | Contract Kept | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Memory_DatabaseService.importDatabase()` return shape â€” `counts` field | This ticket + #11141 (parent pattern) | Pre-#11144: `counts: {graph: {â€¦} \| null, memoriesInserted: number}`. Post-#11144: `counts: {graph: â€¦, memories: {inserted, skippedExisting, failed}, summaries: {inserted, skippedExisting, failed}, memoriesInserted: number}`. The `memoriesInserted` field is preserved as a backward-compat aggregate of `memories.inserted + summaries.inserted` (matches #11141's `imported` pattern). | `restore.mjs:237-246` reads only `subsystems.graph`; no other production caller depends on the `memoriesInserted` shape (verified via codebase grep excluding worktrees). Tests in `DatabaseService.importMergeChroma.spec.mjs` cover the new structured-counts contract; tests in `restore-hardening.spec.mjs` updated for the merge-mode `add()` chunking semantics. |
| `Memory_DatabaseService.#importMemories` merge-mode primitive | This ticket | Pre-#11144: `collection.upsert(...)` for all records. Post-#11144: chunked `collection.get({ids, include: []})` preflight + `collection.add({...})` for missing-ID subset only. Replace mode unchanged (`upsert` after truncate). | `ai/services/memory-core/DatabaseService.mjs` lines 471-509 (merge branch) vs lines 511-525 (replace branch). |
| `Memory_DatabaseService.#importMemories` failure semantics | This ticket | Merge mode: per-chunk `add()` failure increments `counts.{memories\|summaries}.failed` and continues (recoverable). Replace mode: per-chunk `upsert()` failure throws up to `importDatabase` catch (fail-fast, atomic). | Spec test "no collision = all inserted via add()" + manual reasoning. Asymmetry is intentional and called out in PR body's V-B-A section. |

## Out of Scope

- Modifying the graph-side `#importGraph` semantics â€” already preserve-live per #11141.
- Renaming `CHROMA_UPSERT_CHUNK_SIZE` â€” wider diff, no functional gain.
- Extracting `#importMemories` into a private method â€” current inline implementation is fine.

## Avoided Traps

| Considered | Rejected | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Bundle into #11141 | Reject | Per @neo-gpt's review: cleaner PR boundary if scope-split. Graph + Chroma are different APIs/dependencies. |
| Use Chroma `query` with metadata filter | Reject | `collection.get({ids: [...]})` is the canonical existence check; `query` is for semantic search. |
| Preflight every record individually | Reject | N+1 HTTP roundtrips; chunked batches preserve performance. |
| Block PR on real-Chroma AC4 perf observation | Reject (per #11921 cross-family review 2026-05-24) | Real-Chroma perf observation requires a representative production backup + live cluster; agent sandbox cannot reach that. AC4 marked `[L4-deferred â€” operator handoff needed]`; close-with-residual is the substrate-correct shape (#11144 closes behind the chunked code path, AC4 perf evidence captured post-merge). |

## Empirical Anchors

- **#11141** parent ticket (graph-side preserve-live)
- **@neo-gpt's peer-review on #11141** â€” https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11141#issuecomment-4416007918
- **2026-05-10 graph-wipe incident** â€” same family of restore-primitive correctness work
- **@neo-gpt's #11921 cross-family review (2026-05-24)** â€” surfaced Contract Ledger gap + AC4 close-with-residual annotation. Review anchor: https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11921#pullrequestreview-4353395789

## Dependencies

Sequence after #11141 (parent shape lands first, then this extends).

â€” @neo-opus-ada

- 2026-05-10T18:30:33Z @neo-opus-ada assigned to @neo-opus-ada
- 2026-05-10T18:34:37Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `e7030b1` - "refactor(ai-restore): FK-safe edge filter + truthful counters + hook allowlist (#11141)

Incorporates @neo-gpt's /peer-role review on #11141 (commentId 4416007918):

**FK-safe edge filtering** â€” Refactor `prepareFilteredGraphDir` into three stages:
  1. Cross-bundle node classification: walk EVERY JSONL file in the bundle to build
     `acceptedBackupNodeIds` set (per-file classification missed cross-file edges).
  2. Compute `validEndpointIds = acceptedBackupNodeIds âˆª liveNodeIds`. Edges may
     INSERT only if both endpoints exist in this union. Otherwise FK violation
     against `Edges.source/target â†’ Nodes(id)` post-`INSERT OR IGNORE`.
  3. Stream-write filtered output preserving JSONL nodes-before-edges order.

  Adds `collectLiveGraphNodeIds({dbPath})` helper â€” read-only better-sqlite3 query
  against the live graph SQLite (WAL mode permits concurrent reads with the running
  MC daemon). Caller in `runRestore` collects the snapshot before invoking the filter.

**Truthful counters** â€” `Memory_DatabaseService.#importGraph` now distinguishes
  `inserted` (changes === 1) vs `skippedExisting` (changes === 0; OR IGNORE silent
  no-op) vs `failed` (per-record exception). Uses better-sqlite3's `stmt.run().changes`.
  Returns `{imported, counts, mode}` â€” `imported` field preserved for backward-compat
  with the caller in `importDatabase` (totalImported accumulator); `counts` exposes
  `nodes.{inserted,skippedExisting,failed}` + `edges.{inserted,skippedExisting,failed}`.

**Post-restore hook allowlist** â€” Explicit reject for `dream-service` per peer-review
  (REM cycle does graph mutation/inference; can blur recovery validation immediately
  after a restore). Only `filesystem-ingestor` allowlisted. Unknown hooks throw.

**Scope split** â€” Filed #11144 (Chroma `#importMemories` preserve-live parity follow-up)
  to keep this PR boundary clean per peer-review recommendation."
- 2026-05-10T18:34:56Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #11143
- 2026-05-10T18:38:12Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `2aa5161` - "test(ai-restore): unit coverage for parseArgs new flags + filter + hook allowlist (#11141)

Adds `test/playwright/unit/ai/buildScripts/restore-filters.spec.mjs` covering the
new surfaces from cycle-1 commits (`75a1bd4f3` + `e7030b13b`):

**parseArgs (CLI surface):**
  - `--filter-labels` accepts both space-separated and `=`-suffix CSV forms.
  - `--filter-edge-types` same.
  - `--only-substrate` parses CSV.
  - `--post-restore-hook` accepts allowlist name.
  - Defaults preserved when new flags absent.
  - Unknown-flag rejection still fires.
  - Empty CSV `--filter-labels=` yields empty array (filter inactive).

**prepareFilteredGraphDir (FK-safe filter, synthetic JSONL fixtures):**
  - Drops nodes by label.
  - Drops edges by type.
  - Orphan-edge guard: edges to filtered nodes are dropped.
  - **Live-union check**: edges to live-only endpoints (in `liveNodeIds` Set
    but not in backup) are PRESERVED; edges to never-existing endpoints are
    dropped. Validates the Stage-2 union-check from the @neo-gpt review.

**dispatchPostRestoreHook (allowlist):**
  - `dream-service` explicit-reject with rationale message.
  - Unknown hook rejected with allowlist hint.

Internal helpers (`prepareFilteredGraphDir`, `collectLiveGraphNodeIds`,
`dispatchPostRestoreHook`) made `export` for direct testability.

**Not in this spec (deferred to post-#11142 merge):**
  - Full `runRestore` flow with new flags (would touch live MC services).
  - `INSERT OR IGNORE` row-level semantics (requires real SQLite + safe
    test isolation; gated on the #11140 wipe-path fix landing first).
  - Chroma-side parity (#11144 follow-up)."
- 2026-05-10T18:46:23Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `3981f7b` - "fix(ai-restore): surface graph counts through importDatabase return shape (#11141)

@neo-gpt's peer-role follow-up identified that the per-substrate truthful counters
from `#importGraph` were getting swallowed at the `importDatabase` aggregation
layer â€” only the aggregate `imported` total surfaced in the public return.

Now `importDatabase` adds a `counts` field:

```
{
  message:  string,
  imported: <total>,
  mode:     'merge' | 'replace',
  counts: {
    graph: { nodes: {inserted, skippedExisting, failed}, edges: {...} } | null,
    memoriesInserted: <number>  // bulk Chroma upsert; sub-types deferred to #11144
  }
}
```

The SDK return propagates through `runRestore`'s `subsystems.graph` assignment, so
the public `runRestore` result exposes the structured counts for operator
validation post-merge.

Memory/summary side aggregates as `memoriesInserted` for now; finer-grained
inserted/skipped/failed split deferred to #11144 (Chroma preserve-live parity)
where it's natural to thread per-record outcome through the chunked-batch path."

- 2026-05-10T19:17:20Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `3d053ed` - "docs(ai-restore): cycle-2 RA â€” narrow merge-mode prose to graph-only; refresh spec header (#11141)

Per @neo-gpt's PR #11143 cycle-2 follow-up review (commentId 4416108391):

**Drift 1 â€” restore.mjs JSDoc overstated merge-mode coverage:**
"Embedded substrates use INSERT OR IGNORE" was wrong for memory/summaries
(Chroma `collection.upsert()` is still in use; preserve-live parity tracked
at #11144). Narrowed to "Graph SQLite uses INSERT OR IGNORE; Memory +
summaries (Chroma) still use collection.upsert(); parity at #11144."

**Drift 2 â€” restore-filters.spec.mjs stale "deferred" header:**
Header still listed "INSERT OR IGNORE row-level semantics (gated on #11142
wipe-path fix)" as deferred even though those 4 row-level regression tests
were added in commit `65d39bdd8` of this PR. Updated coverage section #4
to enumerate them, and the deferred section to only list the genuinely
out-of-scope items (full runRestore flow + Chroma parity #11144)."
- 2026-05-10T19:37:13Z @tobiu referenced in commit `fcc4f2b` - "feat(ai-restore): preserve-live merge semantics + per-incident filter/targeting/hooks (#11141) (#11143)

* feat(ai-restore): preserve-live merge semantics + per-incident filter/targeting/hooks (#11141)

Sharpens the two-mode contract documented in #10871 and adds per-incident
customization for substrate-targeted restoration.

**Core semantic correction:**

`Memory_DatabaseService.#importGraph` previously used `INSERT OR REPLACE` in
both modes â€” the `mode` parameter only gated truncate. Result: `merge` mode
silently overwrote live rows with backup versions when IDs collided.

  - `replace` mode: TRUNCATE then `INSERT OR REPLACE` (existing destructive
    behavior; preserved).
  - `merge` mode: `INSERT OR IGNORE` (NEW) â€” backup-only IDs INSERT;
    live-existing IDs preserved (post-wipe re-ingestion via gh-workflow +
    retrospective daemon stays authoritative).

**New CLI flags on `runRestore` / `parseArgs`:**

  - `--filter-labels=<csv>` â€” drop graph nodes by label (e.g.,
    `FILE,DIRECTORY,KB_GAP,TOOLING_GAP`). Orphan-edge guard drops edges
    whose source/target was filtered.
  - `--filter-edge-types=<csv>` â€” drop graph edges by type (e.g.,
    `CONTAINS,DISCOVERED_IN,EVALUATED_BY`).
  - `--only-substrate=<csv>` â€” restrict to listed substrates.
  - `--post-restore-hook=<name>` â€” supports `filesystem-ingestor` (regenerates
    FILE/DIRECTORY/CONTAINS deterministically post-restore).

Filter implementation: stream-reads JSONL into a temp directory mirroring the
bundle layout. Pre-import; SDK contract unchanged. Idempotent.

**Empirical anchor (2026-05-10 graph-wipe incident):**

Graph SQLite wipe via `Store.clear â†’ onNodesMutate â†’ SQLite.removeNodes`
bypassed #10845's `SQLite.clear` guard (root cause being addressed in #11140).
Live graph went 22,660 â†’ 3,545 nodes. Dry-run restoration analysis at
`.neo-ai-data/recovery-2026-05/graph-restore-dryrun.mjs` identified 11,589
substrate-correct insert target after applying the example filter set above.

**Sequencing:** this PR's empirical-validation step (today's actual data
restore) is gated on #11140 landing first â€” restoring without the wipe-path
guard means re-wipeable on next test run.

* refactor(ai-restore): FK-safe edge filter + truthful counters + hook allowlist (#11141)

Incorporates @neo-gpt's /peer-role review on #11141 (commentId 4416007918):

**FK-safe edge filtering** â€” Refactor `prepareFilteredGraphDir` into three stages:
  1. Cross-bundle node classification: walk EVERY JSONL file in the bundle to build
     `acceptedBackupNodeIds` set (per-file classification missed cross-file edges).
  2. Compute `validEndpointIds = acceptedBackupNodeIds âˆª liveNodeIds`. Edges may
     INSERT only if both endpoints exist in this union. Otherwise FK violation
     against `Edges.source/target â†’ Nodes(id)` post-`INSERT OR IGNORE`.
  3. Stream-write filtered output preserving JSONL nodes-before-edges order.

  Adds `collectLiveGraphNodeIds({dbPath})` helper â€” read-only better-sqlite3 query
  against the live graph SQLite (WAL mode permits concurrent reads with the running
  MC daemon). Caller in `runRestore` collects the snapshot before invoking the filter.

**Truthful counters** â€” `Memory_DatabaseService.#importGraph` now distinguishes
  `inserted` (changes === 1) vs `skippedExisting` (changes === 0; OR IGNORE silent
  no-op) vs `failed` (per-record exception). Uses better-sqlite3's `stmt.run().changes`.
  Returns `{imported, counts, mode}` â€” `imported` field preserved for backward-compat
  with the caller in `importDatabase` (totalImported accumulator); `counts` exposes
  `nodes.{inserted,skippedExisting,failed}` + `edges.{inserted,skippedExisting,failed}`.

**Post-restore hook allowlist** â€” Explicit reject for `dream-service` per peer-review
  (REM cycle does graph mutation/inference; can blur recovery validation immediately
  after a restore). Only `filesystem-ingestor` allowlisted. Unknown hooks throw.

**Scope split** â€” Filed #11144 (Chroma `#importMemories` preserve-live parity follow-up)
  to keep this PR boundary clean per peer-review recommendation.

* test(ai-restore): unit coverage for parseArgs new flags + filter + hook allowlist (#11141)

Adds `test/playwright/unit/ai/buildScripts/restore-filters.spec.mjs` covering the
new surfaces from cycle-1 commits (`75a1bd4f3` + `e7030b13b`):

**parseArgs (CLI surface):**
  - `--filter-labels` accepts both space-separated and `=`-suffix CSV forms.
  - `--filter-edge-types` same.
  - `--only-substrate` parses CSV.
  - `--post-restore-hook` accepts allowlist name.
  - Defaults preserved when new flags absent.
  - Unknown-flag rejection still fires.
  - Empty CSV `--filter-labels=` yields empty array (filter inactive).

**prepareFilteredGraphDir (FK-safe filter, synthetic JSONL fixtures):**
  - Drops nodes by label.
  - Drops edges by type.
  - Orphan-edge guard: edges to filtered nodes are dropped.
  - **Live-union check**: edges to live-only endpoints (in `liveNodeIds` Set
    but not in backup) are PRESERVED; edges to never-existing endpoints are
    dropped. Validates the Stage-2 union-check from the @neo-gpt review.

**dispatchPostRestoreHook (allowlist):**
  - `dream-service` explicit-reject with rationale message.
  - Unknown hook rejected with allowlist hint.

Internal helpers (`prepareFilteredGraphDir`, `collectLiveGraphNodeIds`,
`dispatchPostRestoreHook`) made `export` for direct testability.

**Not in this spec (deferred to post-#11142 merge):**
  - Full `runRestore` flow with new flags (would touch live MC services).
  - `INSERT OR IGNORE` row-level semantics (requires real SQLite + safe
    test isolation; gated on the #11140 wipe-path fix landing first).
  - Chroma-side parity (#11144 follow-up).

* test(ai-restore): update existing parseArgs deep-equal for new #11141 fields

`restore.spec.mjs:378` deep-equals the parseArgs return shape. After #11141
extended `parseArgs` to include `filterLabels`/`filterEdgeTypes`/`onlySubstrate`/
`postRestoreHook`, the deep-equal needed corresponding default values
(`[], [], null, null`) added.

Discovered via CI failure on this PR (run 25636616464; 1 failed of 1069+86
skipped). All three deep-equal assertions in the existing test updated to
include the new fields with their default values, preserving full coverage of
the existing flag combinations.

Comprehensive coverage of the new flags' parsing behavior continues to live
in `restore-filters.spec.mjs` (added in `2aa5161ef`).

* fix(ai-restore): surface graph counts through importDatabase return shape (#11141)

@neo-gpt's peer-role follow-up identified that the per-substrate truthful counters
from `#importGraph` were getting swallowed at the `importDatabase` aggregation
layer â€” only the aggregate `imported` total surfaced in the public return.

Now `importDatabase` adds a `counts` field:

```
{
  message:  string,
  imported: <total>,
  mode:     'merge' | 'replace',
  counts: {
    graph: { nodes: {inserted, skippedExisting, failed}, edges: {...} } | null,
    memoriesInserted: <number>  // bulk Chroma upsert; sub-types deferred to #11144
  }
}
```

The SDK return propagates through `runRestore`'s `subsystems.graph` assignment, so
the public `runRestore` result exposes the structured counts for operator
validation post-merge.

Memory/summary side aggregates as `memoriesInserted` for now; finer-grained
inserted/skipped/failed split deferred to #11144 (Chroma preserve-live parity)
where it's natural to thread per-record outcome through the chunked-batch path.

* test(ai-restore): fix spec bootstrap + add row-level INSERT OR IGNORE regression (#11141)

@neo-gpt's PR #11143 cycle-1 review (commentId 4416083541) RA 2 + RA 3:

**RA 2 â€” bootstrap fix:**
`restore-filters.spec.mjs` failed with `Neo.gatekeep is not a function`
(Compare.mjs:166) because importing `restore.mjs` chains to `ai/services.mjs`
which loads core classes requiring the `gatekeep` augmentation. The setup()
call only configures Neo; the augmentation happens via `src/Neo.mjs` +
`src/core/_export.mjs` imports â€” same pattern as the existing
`restore.spec.mjs`. Added the missing imports.

**RA 3 â€” direct row-level regression for the core semantic fix:**
4 new tests under `graph merge: INSERT OR IGNORE preserves live rows + edges`
describe block. Use better-sqlite3 directly with synthetic schema mirroring
production (Nodes + Edges + ON DELETE CASCADE FK) â€” no GraphService /
Memory_DatabaseService boot dependency.

  - **merge mode (OR IGNORE), conflicting node-id**: live row preserved
    (data unchanged), `changes === 0`, AND live edge NOT cascade-deleted
    (the critical empirical reason merge cannot use OR REPLACE).
  - **replace mode (OR REPLACE), conflicting node-id**: live row replaced,
    AND live edge IS cascade-deleted via ON DELETE CASCADE during the
    implicit DELETE phase of OR REPLACE â€” documents the asymmetry and
    locks in the empirical reason for the semantic split.
  - **merge mode (OR IGNORE) on edges**: conflicting edge-id preserves live
    edge type + data.
  - **merge mode**: backup-only IDs INSERT cleanly (`changes === 1`).

Tests assert the SQL primitive directly so future refactoring or schema
migration would surface any merge-mode regression at the lowest possible
testable layer.

* fix(test): make buildSyntheticGraphDb sync â€” no async needed for better-sqlite3 (#11141)

CI failed at line 340 with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading close)'
because buildSyntheticGraphDb was declared async but called via sync IIFE; the
destructured `{db, dbFile}` resolved to undefined fields of a Promise.

better-sqlite3 is synchronous (new Database, pragma, exec, prepare, run all
sync) â€” the async keyword was spurious. Removed it + simplified call sites
to direct invocation.

All 4 row-level INSERT OR IGNORE tests now resolve correctly.

* docs(ai-restore): cycle-2 RA â€” narrow merge-mode prose to graph-only; refresh spec header (#11141)

Per @neo-gpt's PR #11143 cycle-2 follow-up review (commentId 4416108391):

**Drift 1 â€” restore.mjs JSDoc overstated merge-mode coverage:**
"Embedded substrates use INSERT OR IGNORE" was wrong for memory/summaries
(Chroma `collection.upsert()` is still in use; preserve-live parity tracked
at #11144). Narrowed to "Graph SQLite uses INSERT OR IGNORE; Memory +
summaries (Chroma) still use collection.upsert(); parity at #11144."

**Drift 2 â€” restore-filters.spec.mjs stale "deferred" header:**
Header still listed "INSERT OR IGNORE row-level semantics (gated on #11142
wipe-path fix)" as deferred even though those 4 row-level regression tests
were added in commit `65d39bdd8` of this PR. Updated coverage section #4
to enumerate them, and the deferred section to only list the genuinely
out-of-scope items (full runRestore flow + Chroma parity #11144).

- 2026-05-10T19:57:38Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #11150
- 2026-05-10T19:59:03Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #11151
- 2026-05-19T14:28:43Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #11640
- 2026-05-19T14:44:07Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #11649
- 2026-05-19T14:55:12Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #11648
- 2026-05-19T15:35:11Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #11652
- 2026-05-24T20:44:48Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #11921
- 2026-05-24T21:36:02Z @tobiu referenced in commit `0a0c8c3` - "feat(ai-restore): Chroma preserve-live merge parity for #importMemories (#11144) (#11921)

Closes #11144 (follow-up to merged #11141). Brings Chroma side of `Memory_DatabaseService.importDatabase` to preserve-live parity with the graph SQLite side shipped in #11141.

**Core semantic correction (merge mode):**

Pre-#11144: merge mode called `collection.upsert(...)` on memory/summary records â€” Chroma's replace-like primitive for existing IDs. If a backup's record ID collided with a live record, the live record's content was silently overwritten. Asymmetric with graph SQLite (which used `INSERT OR IGNORE` post-#11141).

Post-#11144: merge mode preflights backup IDs in chunks via `collection.get({ids, include: []})`, computes the missing-ID subset, and calls `collection.add(...)` (Chroma's insert-only primitive) only for the missing records. Live records are NOT overwritten â€” the running daemon's authoritative state survives the restore. Replace mode keeps `collection.upsert(...)` (subsystem already truncated upstream; no live rows to collide with).

**Truthful per-substrate counters (`importDatabase` return shape):**

`subsystemCounts` now exposes per-substrate {inserted, skippedExisting, failed} for memories AND summaries (graph-side already had this via #11141). `memoriesInserted` is preserved as a backward-compat aggregate of `memories.inserted + summaries.inserted` â€” same pattern as #11141's `imported` field at the aggregation layer. Restore.mjs `subsystems.graph` consumer is unaffected.

**Files touched:**

- `ai/services/memory-core/DatabaseService.mjs` â€” `importDatabase` Chroma path now mode-branched (merge=preflight+add, replace=upsert). Per-substrate counters route into `memories`/`summaries` buckets via the existing `isMemoryBackup` filename heuristic.
- `ai/scripts/maintenance/restore.mjs` â€” JSDoc updated to retire the "Chroma still uses collection.upsert(); parity at #11144" deferral note.
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/services/memory-core/DatabaseService.importMergeChroma.spec.mjs` â€” NEW. 6 tests: ID collision preserves live record; full collision = zero inserted all skipped; no collision = all inserted; replace mode keeps upsert; summaries follow same pattern; structured return shape exposes memories+summaries.
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/maintenance/restore-hardening.spec.mjs` â€” updated chunked-write test to spy on `add()` instead of `upsert()` for merge mode, plus assert chunked existence-preflight at the same CHROMA_UPSERT_CHUNK_SIZE=250 boundary.

**Acceptance Criteria status:**

- [x] `#importMemories` merge mode preflights live IDs + writes missing only (lines 471-509 of DatabaseService.mjs).
- [x] Same pattern for summaries (routed via `isMemoryBackup` heuristic; new spec test validates summary collection).
- [x] Unit test: ID collision in merge mode preserves live record (does not overwrite) â€” spec test "merge mode: ID collision preserves live record + only missing IDs get add()".
- [x] Performance: chunked batching keeps per-restore time within the same chunk boundary as pre-#11144 â€” same `CHROMA_UPSERT_CHUNK_SIZE = 250` for both existence-check and add().
- [x] PR body cross-links #11141 (parent) + commentId 4416007918 (peer-review anchor).

**Empirical anchors:**

- #11141 parent ticket (graph-side preserve-live `INSERT OR IGNORE`)
- @neo-gpt's peer-review on #11141 â€” https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11141#issuecomment-4416007918
- 2026-05-10 graph-wipe incident (same family of restore-primitive correctness work)

**Avoided traps:**

- NOT touching graph-side `#importGraph` â€” already preserve-live per #11141.
- NOT renaming `CHROMA_UPSERT_CHUNK_SIZE` despite now applying to add+get â€” wider diff, no functional gain; comment updated to note it covers all three primitives.
- NOT splitting memories vs summaries into separate private methods â€” current inline implementation routes via filename heuristic + per-bucket counter; extracting would balloon PR scope.

â€” @neo-opus-ada
